headlines.text: US-Iran 2-week ceasefire announced hours before Trump's 8 PM deadline to bomb Iranian cities
Oil crashes 16% as Strait of Hormuz set to reopen; WTI falls below $95 a barrel
Dow surges 1,000+ points, S&P 500 up 2% on ceasefire relief rally
Bitcoin tops $71,000 for first time since March 26 on risk-on surge
Israel says Lebanon NOT included in ceasefire; kills 89 in overnight strikes
Iran launches missile and drone attacks on Kuwait and UAE hours AFTER ceasefire announcement
Pakistan brokered the deal; Islamabad talks with Witkoff and Kushner set for Friday
Elon Musk files to oust OpenAI CEO Sam Altman just weeks before jury selection begins
UK bans Ye (Kanye West) from entering country; Wireless Festival completely canceled
LA teachers strike: 68,000 education workers in 3 unions set to walk

breaking.text: FRAGILE CEASEFIRE: Iran launches missiles at UAE and Kuwait hours after ceasefire deal -- situation remains volatile

stories.hed: US and Iran Agree to Two-Week Ceasefire, But Nobody Can Agree on What It Covers
stories.dek: Trump announced a 'double-sided ceasefire' 90 minutes before his own deadline to bomb Iran's power plants, calling it a massive win. Iran also called it a massive win. Israel said it doesn't apply to Lebanon, Pakistan said it does, and Iran celebrated by firing missiles at Kuwait and the UAE.

stories.hed: Israel Bombs Lebanon With 100 Strikes in 10 Minutes as Ceasefire Debate Rages
stories.dek: Despite the US-Iran ceasefire, Israel pressed on in Lebanon, launching a devastating barrage that killed at least 89 people and wounded over 700 in hours. Netanyahu's office made clear the deal does not cover Hezbollah, and the IDF issued new evacuation orders for Tyre.

stories.hed: Musk Files to Remove OpenAI CEO Altman Weeks Before Trial Begins
stories.dek: Elon Musk escalated his legal war against OpenAI, filing to oust CEO Sam Altman and President Greg Brockman with jury selection weeks away. The legal saga, which could reshape the AI industry, is moving from background noise to courtroom spectacle.

stories.hed: LA Teachers Strike: 68,000 Education Workers in Three Unions Set to Walk
stories.dek: Los Angeles is bracing for a massive teachers strike as 68,000 education workers across three unions prepare to walk off the job, threatening to shut down the nation's second-largest school district. Timing during an already chaotic national news week is, frankly, on-brand.

stories.hed: UK Bans Ye From Entering Britain, Wireless Festival Canceled for Everyone
stories.dek: Britain's Home Office yanked Kanye West's travel authorization, citing his antisemitism as 'not conducive to the public good' -- a UK government sentence that hits harder than any diss track. Sponsors Pepsi and Diageo had already fled, and now tens of thousands of ticket holders are left holding worthless stubs for a festival that lasted exactly as long as his comeback tour deserved.

markets.context: Wall Street erupted in a massive relief rally after Trump announced a 2-week US-Iran ceasefire, sending oil plunging 16% while stocks, crypto, and even gold surged on bets the worst energy shock in history may be pausing.

gas.note: Gas peaked above $4 for the first time since 2022 this week, but the ceasefire has traders dreaming of $3 again -- check back in two weeks when this could all fall apart.

popCulture.hed: UK Bans Ye From Entering Britain, Canceling Wireless Festival and Derailing His Comeback Tour
popCulture.dek: The British Home Office denied Kanye West's travel authorization ahead of his scheduled Wireless Festival headline slot, citing his history of antisemitic statements as 'not conducive to the public good.' The entire three-day London festival was scrapped, leaving tens of thousands of ticket holders -- and Pepsi and Diageo, who had already bailed as sponsors -- wondering why anyone thought this booking was a good idea.

genZ.definition: A math equation that went viral on TikTok starting April 1st after a creator listed random things women find attractive -- and '7x7=49' inexplicably made the cut. Now women are posting jokes about abandoning their partners the moment '7x7=49' walks into the room.
genZ.usage: Trump announced a ceasefire and Wall Street said 'okay but 7x7=49 just walked in' and bought everything anyway.

tldr: The US and Iran agreed to a fragile two-week ceasefire hours before Trump's apocalyptic deadline, sending oil prices cratering 16% and sparking the biggest market rally in months -- though Iran immediately celebrated by firing missiles at Kuwait and the UAE, so 'ceasefire' is doing some heavy lifting as a word. Meanwhile Kanye West got banned from Britain, 68,000 LA teachers are about to strike, and Elon Musk is trying to fire Sam Altman, because apparently one civilization-threatening crisis at a time is no longer the vibe.
